text.p: parts of Lower South East district.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Move vehicles under cover or away from trees;
* Secure or put away loose items around your property.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while conditions are severe.

warning-info.text: The next Severe Weather Warning will be issued by 5:00 pm ACST Thursday.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Damaging winds over coastal parts of the Lower South East from this evening, easing Friday morning.

text.p: Weather Situation: Increased southwesterly flow is forecast from this evening as a low pressure system passes to the south of South Australia. Winds ease during Friday morning as the system weakens and moves away to the east.
DAMAGING WEST TO SOUTHWESTERLY WINDS averaging 50 to 65 km/h with peak gusts of around 100 km/h are likely over the Lower South East coast during this evening before easing during Friday morning.
Locations which may be affected include Robe and Millicent.
DAMAGING WEST TO SOUTHWESTERLY WINDS averaging 50 to 65 km/h with peak gusts of around 100 km/h are likely over the Lower South East coast during this evening before easing during Friday morning.
Locations which may be affected include Robe and Millicent.
